violations.text: Bottles of juice were observed stored in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Staff removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026

Juice and yogurt were observed held at 48 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Yogurt and melon (prepared in morning) were moved to other refrigeration. Juice was disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026

Sandwiches, fruit cups, and salads packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Guidance provided. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2026

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of potatoes. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Bowl was removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026

The open-air/display case was in poor repair, holding an ambient temperature of 48-54 degrees. Per PIC, unit is scheduled for replacement during current renovations. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/08/2025

The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. PIC informed inspector that housekeeping staff receive training, but do not have written procedures. Inspector provided RIDOH guidance. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machines) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Maintenace was notified, ice machines taken out of service, ice to be disposed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2025

Yogurt was observed held at 51 degrees Fahrenheit in the service case. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2025
Cheese was observed stored out of packaging (fell out) in walk-in. PIC disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2025

Service display case was not in good repair, observed with an ambient temperature of 49 degrees. Foods recently placed in unit were moved to other refrigeration. Foods that had been in unit for an unknown period of time were disposed. Service called. New Violation. Correct By: 07/08/2025

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arrranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Raw animal foods (such as liquid eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as fruit) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machines on guest floors) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machines taken out of service during inspection. Ice machines must be thoroughly clean and sanitized prior to use. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024

Breakfast burritos, guac, cream cheese, cut fruit, parfaits and juices were observed held at (44-51) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods out of temperature for an unknown amount of time were voluntarily disposed of. Foods that were recently place were relocated to other refrigeration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024
Utensils were observed stored in between prep unit and prep table between uses. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024

Prep unit and display case located in the front service area were not in good repair. Units were taken out of service to hold TCS/PHF during time of inspection. Ambient temperatures of 45 and 48 degrees Fahrenheit. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2024

The receptacle in the public women's toilet room is lacking a cover. A toilet room used by females shall be provided with a covered receptacle for sanitary napkins. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2024
